communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All reports of NIL deals for student athletes between Jan 1, 2025, and April 8, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Madison Burrows

To Whom It May Concern:

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bernadette Feazell

CJ Ciaramella

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Camp East Montana records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All use-of-force incident reports, use-of-force logs, video recordings of use-of-force incidents , use-of-force review board findings, and after-action reports for Camp East Montana from July 1, 2025, to the date of your search.

2. All Detainee Death Reports (DDRs), medical records summaries, mortality review reports, and any other records relating to deaths in custody at Camp East Montana from July 1, 2025, to the date of your search.

3. The complete corrective action plan (CAP) submitted in response to the ICE Office of Detention Oversight Inspection No. 2026-001-098 (conducted February 10-12, 2026).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

CJ Ciaramella

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Gabriel Garcia-Aviles ICE detention records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All electronic health records pertaining to Gabriel Garcia-Aviles, a 56-year-old Mexican national who died on Oct. 23, 2025, while in ICE custody at the Adelanto ICE Processing Center. This include medical encounter records, medical intake screenings, sick call requests, treatment notes, and any other medical or health-related documentation pertaining to Mr. Garcia-Aviles during his detention.

3. All Detainee Death Reports and mortality review reports related to Mr. Garcia-Aviles' death.

2. All administrative grievances filed by or on behalf of Mr. Garcia-Aviles during his detention, including any responses or disposition records.

Proof of death is not required, as Garcia-Aviles' death has been widely reported, including in an ICE press release: https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/illegal-alien-dies-victorville-medical-center-california-after-complications-alcohol

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

CJ Ciaramella

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA - Fox - US NAVY Aviator
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) for Mark I. Fox
Request copy of history of assignments NAVPERS form 1070/605, Legion of Merit award citations, Meritorious Service medal citation, Bronze Star Medal citation, Navy Commendation medal citations, navy achievement medal citations, Air Medal award citations, all award citations, Cumulative Record of Training (NAVPERS Form 5300/16), Transfers and Receipts NAVPERS 601-12 from 1970 to 2000, Administrative remarks NAVPERS 1070/613, dates of promotions.
Please redact any personal information per Freedom of Information Act, U.S.C. § 552(b)(3) and U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).

VETERAN INFORMATION
Name of veteran: Mark I. Fox
Branch of service: United States Navy
Rank: VADM
Service: 1978 to 2016

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
